zotero.abstractNote: The inference of population structure is a fundamental task in population genetics. Most existing methods rely on hard clustering, assigning each individual to a single ancestral population. However, this approach fails to capture the continuous nature of genetic variation and the complex relationships between populations. We introduce tangleGen, a hierarchical soft clustering approach that models genetic data as a mixture of ancestral populations. tangleGen is based on a hierarchical model that allows for the representation of population structure at different levels of granularity. We demonstrate the effectiveness of tangleGen on simulated and real-world datasets, showing that it outperforms existing methods in capturing population structure and identifying ancestral relationships. tangleGen is implemented as an R package and is freely available.

notes.note: Publication Title: bioRxiv (Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory) Publisher: University of Tübingen
